enonce.texte: Match each school subject to its correct picture or definition. Write the correct letter (A, B, C, etc.) in the box next to each subject.
images: A simple line drawing of a calculator and a ruler for Maths
A simple line drawing of an open book with a pencil for English
A simple line drawing of a globe for Geography
A simple line drawing of a test tube for Science
A simple line drawing of a paint palette for Art

questions.texte: Match the words to their corresponding definitions:

sousQuestions.reponse: d. Subject with numbers and calculations
a. Subject where we read books and write stories
e. Subject with experiments and labs
b. Subject that studies the past
c. Subject where we draw and paint

enonce.texte: Look at the picture of a classroom. Identify the objects mentioned in the questions.
images: A simple cartoon drawing of a classroom with a teacher's desk at the front, a blackboard on the wall, several student desks, chairs, a bookcase with books, a large clock on the wall, and a bin in the corner.

questions.texte: What object is used for writing on the wall?
Where do students usually sit and write?
What do you use to write in your notebook?
What tells you the time in the classroom?

enonce.texte: Here is the school timetable for Class 6A. Study it carefully and answer the questions below.
images: A simple weekly timetable table with rows for periods (1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th) and columns for days (Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday). The periods are filled with subjects: Monday (Maths, English, History, PE), Tuesday (Science, Art, English, Maths), Wednesday (English, Maths, Geography, Art), Thursday (PE, Science, History, English), Friday (Maths, English, Science, Art).

questions.texte: Analyze the timetable and answer these questions:

sousQuestions.texte: How many English lessons are there in a week?
sousQuestions.reponse: There are 4 English lessons in a week.

sousQuestions.texte: Which school subject is on the timetable every day?
What is the last period on Wednesday?
How many days does the school week include?

enonce.texte: Rearrange the words to form correct sentences about school subjects and objects.

sousQuestions.reponse: My books are in my backpack.

enonce.texte: Read the clues and find the correct classroom object.

questions.texte: I am used for writing on the board. What am I?
I am a flat surface where you can put your books and papers. What am I?
I show the days and hours of school. What am I?

enonce.texte: Design a weekly timetable for a student in Class 6. You must include the following subjects: English, Maths, Science, History, Geography, PE, and Art. You also have a 30-minute break each morning. Write at least 4 sentences describing your timetable and explain your choices.

questions.texte: Describe your timetable and explain your choices.

enonce.texte: You are going back to school. Write a short dialogue (3-4 exchanges) between you and a shopkeeper where you buy a new school bag, some books, and a pen. Your dialogue must use the vocabulary of classroom objects and school subjects.

questions.texte: Write the dialogue and prepare to present it to the class.
